hero.description: A compromised key doesn't mean total loss. I specialize in <highlight>rescuing assets from sweeper-compromised wallets</highlight> across all EVM chains — including locked staking positions, pending airdrops, and vesting contracts.

step01.title: Submit Your Wallet
step01.description: Provide the private key of your compromised wallet. The key is <highlight>encrypted on the server</highlight> before storage and cannot be read by anyone except me. Your wallet address is automatically derived from the key.

step02.title: Ownership Verification
step02.description: The first funding transaction of your wallet is automatically traced across all EVM chains. If it was funded from a known exchange, you'll be asked to upload the original withdrawal email (.eml) for <highlight>cryptographic verification</highlight>.

step03.title: Asset Rescue
step03.description: Once ownership is confirmed, a counter-sweeper strategy is executed to extract your assets — staked tokens, pending airdrops, vesting contracts — and transfer them to your designated safe wallet before the malicious bot can react.

login.title: Initiate Rescue
login.subtitle: Secure Whitehat Portal Access
login.sessionNote:
Encrypted session established.

All credentials are securely protected.

initError.defaultMessage: Failed to load dashboard. Please check your connection and try again.

safeWallet.setDescription: All recovered assets will be sent to this address. Make sure it is a wallet you fully control.
safeWallet.warning: This will be the permanent destination for all rescued funds. You can only change this address once every 3 days. For urgent changes, contact @codeesura or contact@codeesura.dev.
safeWallet.cexWarning: DO NOT enter a CEX (exchange) address. Rescued funds sent to exchange deposit addresses (Binance, Coinbase, etc.) may be permanently lost. Only use a self-custody wallet (MetaMask, Ledger, Rabby, etc.).
safeWallet.invalidAddress: Invalid Ethereum address. Must start with 0x followed by 40 hex characters.
safeWallet.changeDescription: Enter your new safe wallet address.
safeWallet.changeCexWarning: DO NOT enter a CEX (exchange) address. Funds sent to exchange deposit addresses may be permanently lost. Only use a wallet you fully control (MetaMask, Ledger, etc.).
safeWallet.label: Safe Wallet
safeWallet.infoText: Address changes are limited to once every 3 days. For urgent requests, contact me.

submitWallet.description: Enter the private key of your compromised wallet. Your wallet address will be automatically detected. The key is encrypted before storage and can only be accessed by me for rescue operations.
submitWallet.invalidKeyHint: Enter a valid EVM private key (64 hex characters). Mnemonic phrases are not accepted.
submitWallet.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.

fundingUnavailable.description: The funding source of this wallet could not be automatically detected. This may happen with bridge transactions, contract deployments, or non-standard funding methods. Please contact me directly for manual verification.

manualVerification.description: This wallet was not funded from a recognized exchange. Automatic email verification is not available for this wallet. Please contact me with any proof of ownership (transaction history, signing a message, or other evidence) so I can proceed with your rescue request.

emlRequired.description: This wallet was funded from {cex}. Upload the original withdrawal confirmation email in .eml format.

emlGuide.title: How to download .eml file
emlGuide.step1: Open your email inbox and find the withdrawal confirmation email from {cex} for this specific transaction.
emlGuide.gmail: Gmail: Open email → ⋮ (three dots, top right) → "Download message"
emlGuide.outlook: Outlook: Open email → File → Save As → save as .eml
emlGuide.step3: Upload the downloaded .eml file below. Our system will automatically verify it.
emlGuide.dkimWarning: Do not forward the email to yourself or anyone before downloading. Forwarding breaks the DKIM signature and the file will be rejected. Always download the original email directly from your inbox.

verified.description: DKIM signature verified successfully. Your wallet has been added to the rescue queue. Your wallet is in the rescue queue. I will execute the rescue strategy as soon as possible. No further action is needed from you.

rejected.description: The uploaded email could not be verified. This may happen if the email was forwarded, modified, or not downloaded in the original .eml format. Please contact me for manual review.

AuthError.title: Something went wrong
AuthError.description: We could not complete the authentication process. This may happen if the login session expired or was interrupted. Please try again.

Metadata.title: Whitehat Rescue Ops // Secure Asset Recovery
Metadata.description: Advanced counter-sweeper strategies and secure asset extraction for compromised EVM wallets.

NotFound.title: Page Not Found
NotFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
